author: Egor Andreevich
message:
[Gradle] Append Playwright runner name to test name in IDE

This change fixes PlaywrightTestExecutor to match the behaviour
of the Karma executor, where the information about the browser
and OS a test is running on is appended to the test name for
reporting. In case of Playwright, the runner name, as specified
in the DSL, is appended instead. So for an unnamed Chromium
runner, Karma would report the test name as follows:

test[js, browser, Chrome150.0.0.0, MacOS10.15.7]

whereas Playwright reports the following:

test[js, browser, chromium]

^KT-86799 Verification Pending

author: Andrey Mogilev
message: [Kapt/Maven] support new option `kapt.stub.generation.scheme`

author: Andrey Mogilev
message: [Kapt/Gradle] support new Kapt option `stubGenerationScheme`

author: Andrey Mogilev
message:
[Kapt] add optional direct stub generation scheme

With a new Kapt option `-stubGenerationScheme=direct|jtree`, the
text-based "direct" scheme of stub generation is introduced. It does
not use javac's JTree API, and thus eventually will allow dropping that
dependency.
In the current implementation, both schemes are executed in parallel.

^KT-71883 Fixed

# Conflicts:

#	plugins/kapt/kapt-compiler/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/kapt/stubs/KaptStubConverter.kt

author: Oleg Makeev
message:
[Analysis API] Support `fakeOverrideOriginal` for all `KaCallableSymbol`

Previously, the API only worked for top-level callables:
functions, properties, fields, etc.
But for parameters, receivers, accessors and other sub-callables of fake overrides, nothing was unwrapped.
That's because the compiler only puts the corresponding attributes on top-level callables and doesn't propagate them to sub-callables.

With these changes, `fakeOverrideOriginal` is now able to unwrap sub-callables as well.
E.g., for a `KaValueParameterSymbol` of some fake override function, the API returns the corresponding value parameter in the original function.

^KT-69959 fixed

author: Oleg Makeev
message:
[FIR] Adjust setter parameter copying in `FirFakeOverrideGenerator`

Value/context parameters of fake overrides generally have the same source as the enclosing fake override callable.
This can be observed in `buildCopyForValueParameter` calls.
Usually this source is the containing derived class.
However, value parameters of setters didn't follow these rules.
Instead, the source was the same as the source of the original overriden parameters.
For implicit default setters, the source was the original property.
For custom setters, the source was the original value parameter.

This change adjusts the source handling for setter value parameters.
Now the default source for these parameters is the source of the enclosing fake override.

^KT-69959

author: Oleg Makeev
message:
[Analysis API] Support context parameters in `KaSymbolByFirBuilderKt.collectReferencedTypeParameters`

`collectReferencedTypeParameters` is used to decide whether `SUBSTITUTION_OVERRIDE` wrappers should be unwrapped in `KaSymbolByFirBuilder`.
If the wrapper has the same signature as the overriden symbol, the overriden symbol is used instead.
This is calculated based on the set of type parameters used in the overriden symbol.
If these type parameters are not substituted in the wrapper, then there is no need in keeping the wrapper.

However, the logic completely ignored type parameters used in context parameters.
In cases when type parameters were only present in context parameters, wrappers were considered identical to their overriden symbols even these parameters were truly substituted.

^KT-69959

author: Oleg Makeev
message:
[FIR] Adjust context parameter copying in `FirFakeOverrideGenerator`

Generally, sub-callables of fake overrides (e.g., value parameters) are consistent with their corresponding fake overrides:
- Origin is set to `DELEGATED` / `INTERSECTION_OVERRIDE` / `SUBSTITUTION_OVERRIDE`
- Containing declaration of sub-callables inside some fake override is this override callable

But this rule didn't apply to context parameters of fake overrides produced by `FirFakeOverrideGenerator`.
They were an exact copy of their origins except for the return type.
This led to context parameters having `SOURCE` origin and the original callables as a containing declaration.

Context parameter copying is now unified with value parameter copying.
Copied context parameters have correct origins and containing declarations.

^KT-69959

author: Radoslaw Panuszewski
message:
[Build] Mark tests in `kotlin-gradle-plugin-dsl-codegen` as smoke tests

It is necessary because changes in other domains (like Native) may require updating the generated sources. When Test Federation is enabled, such changes would quietly reach master without running codegen tests.

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: minor, remove unneeded KClass.getFakeOverrideMembersByName

It was always invoked on the result of `KType.classifier`, which is
never `MutableCollectionKClass`.

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: inline MutableMap.mergeWith in fakeOverrides.kt

To make it a bit clearer what exactly happens here.

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: do not load javaMethod when computing EquatableCallableSignature

Instead, extract JVM method name/signature from
`ReflectKFunction.signature`. And load generic parameter types from
`javaMethod` only when necessary (i.e. when `equalityMode` is
`JavaSignature`).

 #KT-80710

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: simplify EquatableCallableSignature.equals

Do not recreate types to get rid of flexible nullability/mutability.
`areEqualKTypes` works correctly for flexible types, and the same logic
is used in the fake override builder in the compiler code (there's no
"type recreation" there). Also, this somehow affected signature equality
in presence of DNN types, which now results in kotlin-reflect behavior
being closer to the compiler in a few tests.

Also, adjust the `isKotlin` check: previously we incorrectly detected
mapped builtin classes to be "not Kotlin" because their `Class` objects
lacked the `@kotlin.Metadata` annotation. Mapped builtin classes have
metadata in another place (`.kotlin_builtins`). Now we check if
`kmClass` (loaded either from the annotation, or from the
kotlin_builtins files) is not null. This doesn't affect any existing
tests, but makes sure that the correct set of signature checks in
`EquatableCallableSignature.equals` is run for them, namely
Kotlin-based, not Java-based, ones.

 #KT-80710

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: change the logic of resolving function references

Previously, to resolve a reference to a Java function given its
coordinates (name, container, signature), we looked up the corresponding
`Method` instance in the class and its supertypes, and then created a
`JavaKNamedFunction` over it.

Now, instead of this, we resolve all functions with this name in the
class, and select the one with the correct signature.

The main reason to do this is to support references to inherited
functions in the new implementation. To create the `JavaKNamedFunction`
instance, we need the correct data in `KCallableOverriddenStorage`.
(`EMPTY` was incorrect because it lacked type substitution and other
data, which led to incorrect behavior in KT-86926.)

In the legacy K1 implementation, resolution of a reference started with
looking up the corresponding descriptor in the member scope (see
`KDeclarationContainerImpl.findFunctionDescriptor`), whose underlying
implementation lazily resolves all functions with the given name. Since
there's no concept of "member scope" in the new implementation, we have
to repeat that logic here.

Note that to create a bound function reference (the case when
`getBoundReceiver()` is not null), we now need to copy the unbound
reference obtained from `getMembersByName` and provide the bound
receiver, which is done via the recently introduced `rebind`.

 #KT-80710
 #KT-86926 Fixed

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: compute and cache declared/all members of KClass by name

In the legacy K1 implementation, member scopes already cached
descriptors by name. In the new implementation, there's no concept of
member scope, so supporting Kotlin class members would lead to a
performance regression when resolving callable references to such
members.

Namely, when we try to resolve a callable reference (see e.g.
`ReflectionFactoryImpl.function`) given its coordinates (name,
container, signature), we'd need to compute _all_ members of the class
first. Unfortunately, finding the necessary `KmFunction` and creating a
`KFunction` (like it's being done right now for top-level functions)
wouldn't work for inherited functions.

With this change, resolution of callable references to class members,
which is coming in a subsequent commit, can use the new
`getMembersByName` function, which would only compute and cache members
with the given name, same as it was done in K1.

 #KT-80710

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Alexander Udalov
message:
Reflection: unbind KProperty correctly

Add a new function, `rebind`, instead of `shallowCopy`, to bind
receivers, change the bound receiver, or unbind all receivers.

This change originally started as a prerequisite for refactoring
property reference creation, where the main idea is: instead of trying
to determine what kind of property we're dealing with (Kotlin, Java,
synthetic enum entries, new impl, old impl, etc.), find the property by
name in its container's `members`, and then _bind_ the receiver, if it's
present. While implementing bind for properties, I found the bug
KT-87308 in the existing function `unbindAllReceivers`, which was used
to fix KT-85025. Apparently, to bind/unbind/rebind property we might
need to create a property of a different arity, so simple `shallowCopy`
will not work.

Note that there are only two use cases:
1) Unbind all receivers, which is used to fix KT-85025 & KT-87308
2) Bind receiver, which will be used by the upcoming refactoring of
   property reference creation. There's no way in the language to bind
   multiple receivers yet, so it's only possible to bind one, dispatch
   or extension receiver. There might be some changes due to KT-86452 /
   KT-8835 in the future though.

There's no use case for "rebinding" receiver while keeping arity, but
I've still supported this case because it's trivial.

 #KT-87308 Fixed
